     The Numbers Business Report

Cirque du Soleil: Worlds Away (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $12,512,862Details
International Box Office $21,640,239
Worldwide Box Office $34,153,101
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,251,252
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,377,110
Total $4,628,362
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A young couple who is separated, must journey through the astonishing and dreamlike worlds of Cirque du Soleil to find each other, as audiences experience the immersive 3D technology that will allow them to leap, soar, swim, and dance with the performers.  

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$2,282,368 (18.2% of total gross)
Legs:5.21 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:36.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:840 opening theaters/840 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $18,661,798

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 21st, 2012 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
Video Release: March 12th, 2013 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for some dramatic images and mild sensuality.
(Rating bulletin 2252, 12/12/2012)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Franchise: Cirque du Soleil
Cast, crew, or production detail: Limited Dialogue, Unnamed Character
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Acrobatics, Circus, Love at First Sight, Romance
Subgenre: Chase Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: James Cameron, Cirque du Soleil, Reel FX, Strange Weather, Cameron/Pace Group
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
